recipes.title: Truffle & Forest Mushroom Risotto
recipes.description: A creamy, decadent Arborio rice dish infused with aromatic forest mushrooms, white wine, and finished with luxurious black truffle oil and freshly grated Parmesan cheese.
instructions: Sauté shallots and garlic in butter in a large pan until soft and fragrant.
Add mushrooms and thyme, cooking until mushrooms release their moisture and brown.
Stir in the Arborio rice, toasting the grains for 2 minutes until translucent at the edges.
Pour in the white wine and simmer, stirring constantly, until it is completely absorbed.
Begin adding the warm broth one ladle at a time, stirring continuously.
Once rice is al dente, stir in the Parmesan cheese, cover and let rest for 2 minutes.
Drizzle with truffle oil and serve immediately.

recipes.title: Creamy Paneer Butter Masala
recipes.description: A popular, rich and creamy North Indian curry made with soft paneer cubes cooked in a velvety tomato-butter gravy spiced with sweet aromatics.
instructions: Blend the soaked cashews with a little water into a smooth, creamy paste and set aside.
Melt half the butter in a pan, add chopped onions and sauté until translucent.
Stir in the ginger-garlic paste and cook for 1 minute until the raw aroma disappears.
Add tomato puree, Kashmiri red chili powder, and salt. Simmer for 8-10 minutes until oil separates.
Stir in the cashew paste and cook for 2 more minutes.
Add paneer cubes, garam masala, and 1/2 cup of warm water. Simmer on low heat for 5 minutes.
Finish by crushing kasuri methi between your palms into the curry, stir in fresh cream and the remaining butter. Garnish with coriander.

reviews.comment: Matches the taste of best Delhi dhabas! Easy to follow.

recipes.title: Classic Chicken Tikka Masala
recipes.description: Juicy pieces of chicken tikka grilled and simmered in an aromatic, vibrant tomato cream sauce with fragrant Indian spices.
instructions: Marinate chicken cubes in yogurt, 1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste, 1 tsp garam masala, 1 tsp red chili, salt, and lemon juice for at least 20 minutes.
Sauté marinated chicken in a hot pan with butter for 6-8 minutes until slightly charred on the edges, then set aside.
In the same pan, cook chopped onions until soft. Add remaining ginger-garlic paste, garam masala, and chili powder.
Pour in tomato puree and simmer for 10 minutes until the sauce reduces and thickens.
Add the cooked chicken tikka pieces back into the gravy and simmer for 5 minutes.
Pour in heavy cream and stir well. Garnish with chopped fresh coriander leaves and serve warm with naan.

reviews.comment: A true crowd pleaser. Tastes incredibly authentic!

recipes.title: Avocado Smash with Poached Eggs
recipes.description: Rich sourdough toast topped with seasoned smashed avocado, perfectly poached eggs, feta crumbles, and sprinkled with Egyptian Dukkah spice blend.
instructions: In a bowl, smash avocado with lemon juice, salt, pepper, and chili flakes until chunky-smooth.
Toast the sourdough bread slices until golden brown.
Poach the eggs in simmering water with a splash of vinegar for exactly 3 minutes.
Spread the smashed avocado generously over the toasted bread slices.
Carefully place a poached egg on top of each avocado toast.
Crumble feta cheese over the top, then garnish with Dukkah spice and microgreens.

recipes.title: Egg curry
recipes.description: A chef-crafted, premium version of your requested dish: egg curry, refined for your None specifications.
instructions: Prep the core items needed for your egg curry.
Heat butter or olive oil in a suitable pan.
Toss in the main and supporting elements, searing until golden-brown.
Season thoroughly with fresh herbs and spices.
Garnish creatively and serve warm.

recipes.title: Authentic Spaghetti Carbonara
recipes.description: A classic Roman pasta dish made with spaghetti, crispy guanciale, creamy egg yolks, freshly grated Pecorino Romano, and cracked black pepper.
instructions: Bring a large pot of water (about 3 liters) to a rolling boil. Add salt, then add spaghetti. Cook until 1 minute before al dente (check pasta packaging for time).
While pasta cooks, heat a large skillet over medium heat. Add guanciale cubes and cook for 5-7 minutes until the fat renders and guanciale becomes crispy and golden. Turn off heat.
In a medium bowl, whisk egg yolks and the whole egg together. Stir in grated Pecorino Romano and cracked black pepper until it forms a thick, creamy paste.
Reserve 1/2 cup of hot pasta cooking water, then drain the spaghetti.
Add the hot drained spaghetti directly to the skillet containing guanciale and rendered fat. Toss well for 1 minute off the heat to cool slightly (to avoid scrambling the eggs).
Pour the egg-cheese mixture over the pasta. Toss and stir rapidly. Add 2-3 tablespoons of the reserved pasta water to emulsify the cheese and grease into a smooth, glossy sauce.
Continue tossing off the heat until a thick, creamy sauce coats all spaghetti strands. Serve immediately topped with extra Pecorino and cracked pepper.

recipes.title: Korean style maggie
recipes.description: A detailed, beginner-friendly recipe for preparing a delicious korean style maggie, customized to a None diet.
instructions: Prepare all ingredients: finely chop onions, mince garlic, and slice main cooking ingredients into uniform, bite-sized pieces for even cooking.
Heat olive oil or butter in a medium-sized skillet over medium heat for 1 minute until shimmering.
Add chopped onions and minced garlic to the skillet. Sauté on medium-low heat for 3-4 minutes, stirring regularly, until fragrant and onions turn soft and translucent.
Increase heat slightly to medium. Add the main ingredients and sauté for 6-8 minutes, tossing frequently, until cooked through and lightly browned on the edges.
Sprinkle the spices, salt, and pepper evenly over the skillet contents. Stir thoroughly for 1 minute to release spice oils and coat the food.
Reduce heat to low, cover the pan, and simmer for 3 minutes to allow flavors to meld. Garnish with fresh herbs and serve immediately.

recipes.title: Homestyle Butter Chicken (हिंदी अनुवाद)
recipes.description: Tender pieces of marinated chicken grilled to smoky perfection and simmered in a rich, buttery, spiced tomato-cream gravy. [हिंदी रूपांतरण]
instructions: 1. In a bowl, combine chicken cubes with yogurt, 1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste, 1 tsp garam masala, 1 tsp Kashmiri red chili, salt, and lemon juice. Marinate for 20 minutes. (विधि)

2. Melt 10g of butter in a large skillet over high heat. Add the marinated chicken pieces and sear for 3-4 minutes on each side until cooked through and slightly charred on the edges. Remove chicken and set aside. (विधि)
3. In the same skillet, melt the remaining butter (15g). Sauté finely chopped onions for 5 minutes on medium heat until golden. (विधि)
4. Add the remaining ginger-garlic paste, garam masala, and red chili powder. Sauté for 1 minute until fragrant. (विधि)
5. Add tomato puree and salt. Reduce heat to medium-low and simmer for 10 minutes until the tomato gravy thickens and darkens in color. (विधि)
6. Stir in the grilled chicken tikka pieces. Simmer uncovered for 5 minutes to let the chicken absorb the curry flavor. (विधि)
7. Lower the heat to low, stir in heavy cream, and simmer for another 2 minutes. Garnish with chopped fresh coriander leaves and serve. (विधि)

recipes.title: Classic Italian Tiramisu
recipes.description: An elegant, authentic Italian dessert layered with coffee-dipped ladyfingers, rich mascarpone cream, and dusted with fine cocoa powder.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk the heavy cream, sugar, and vanilla extract together until stiff peaks form.
Gently fold the mascarpone cheese into the whipped cream until smooth and combined. Be careful not to overmix.
Quickly dip each ladyfinger cookie into the cooled espresso for 1-2 seconds (do not soak them) and arrange them in a single layer at the bottom of an 8x8 inch dish.
Spread half of the mascarpone cream mixture evenly over the ladyfinger layer.
Dip the remaining ladyfingers in espresso and place them in a second layer on top of the cream.
Spread the remaining mascarpone cream on top and smooth the surface with a spatula.
Dust the top generously with cocoa powder. Refrigerate for at least 4 hours (ideally overnight) before slicing and serving.

recipes.title: Homemade Margherita Pizza
recipes.description: A classic Neapolitan-style pizza with a hand-stretched thin crust, rich tomato sauce, fresh mozzarella, and fragrant basil leaves.
instructions: Preheat your oven to its maximum temperature (250°C / 480°F) for at least 30 minutes with a pizza stone or baking tray inside.
Mix tomato passata with minced garlic, a pinch of salt, and dried oregano to make the sauce. Set aside.
On a lightly floured surface, stretch or roll the pizza dough into a 10-12 inch round, keeping the edges slightly thicker.
Transfer the dough to a piece of parchment paper. Spread the tomato sauce evenly leaving a 1-inch border for the crust.
Tear the fresh mozzarella and distribute it evenly over the sauce. Drizzle with 1 tbsp of olive oil.
Slide the pizza (on parchment) onto the hot baking stone. Bake for 10-13 minutes until the crust is golden and the cheese is bubbling and lightly browned.
Remove from oven, immediately scatter fresh basil leaves, drizzle remaining olive oil, slice into 6 pieces, and serve hot.

recipes.title: Chole Bhature
recipes.description: Indian style Chole Bhature prepared in 105 minutes.
instructions: **For Chole (Chickpea Curry):**
1. Wash and soak white chickpeas (Kabuli Chana) in plenty of water overnight, or for at least 8-10 hours. Drain and rinse the soaked chickpeas.

2. In a pressure cooker, combine the soaked chickpeas, 4 cups of fresh water, 2 tea bags, 1/4 teaspoon baking soda, and 1 teaspoon of salt. Pressure cook for 5-6 whistles on medium-high heat, then reduce heat and cook for another 10-15 minutes until the chickpeas are very tender. Let the pressure release naturally. Remove the tea bags and set aside the boiled chickpeas with their cooking liquid.

3. Heat 3 tablespoons of cooking oil in a large heavy-bottomed pan or kadai over medium heat. Add cumin seeds, bay leaf, green cardamom, black cardamom, cinnamon stick, and cloves. Sauté for 30 seconds until fragrant.
4. Add the finely chopped onion and sauté until golden brown (about 8-10 minutes).
5. Add ginger-garlic paste and slit green chilies. Sauté for 1-2 minutes until the raw smell disappears.
6. Pour in the tomato puree. Cook, stirring occasionally, until the oil starts to separate from the masala (about 8-10 minutes).
7. Add coriander powder, cumin powder, turmeric powder, and red chili powder. Mix well and cook for 1-2 minutes, adding a splash of water if the masala sticks to the bottom.
8. Stir in the chana masala powder, dry mango powder (amchur), and the remaining 1/2 teaspoon of salt. Mix thoroughly.

9. Add the boiled chickpeas along with their cooking liquid to the pan. Gently mash a few chickpeas with the back of your ladle to thicken the gravy. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat to low and simmer, covered, for 15-20 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld.
10. Crush the dried fenugreek leaves (Kasuri Methi) between your palms and add to the chole. Stir well. Taste and adjust salt or spices if needed.
11. Garnish with fresh chopped coriander leaves before serving.
**For Bhature (Fried Bread):**
1. In a large mixing bowl, combine all-purpose flour, semolina, sugar, 1/2 teaspoon salt, and baking powder. Mix well.

2. Add yogurt to the dry ingredients and mix. Gradually add water, a little at a time, and knead to form a soft, smooth, and elastic dough. The dough should be slightly sticky initially, but continue kneading for 8-10 minutes until it becomes smooth. You might not need all the water.
3. Add 2 tablespoons of cooking oil to the dough and knead for another 2-3 minutes until the oil is fully incorporated and the dough is very soft and pliable.
4. Cover the dough with a damp cloth or plastic wrap and let it rest in a warm place for at least 2-3 hours (or even longer, up to 4-5 hours for better fermentation and fluffiness).
5. After resting, gently punch down the dough and knead for another minute. Divide the dough into 8-10 equal portions and roll them into smooth balls.

6. Heat 4 cups of cooking oil for deep frying in a deep kadai or heavy-bottomed pan over medium-high heat. The oil should be hot but not smoking (test by dropping a small piece of dough; it should sizzle and rise quickly).

7. Lightly grease your rolling surface and rolling pin with a little oil. Take one dough ball and gently roll it into an oval or round shape, about 6-7 inches in diameter and slightly thicker than a roti. Do not use dry flour for dusting, as it can burn the oil.

8. Carefully slide the rolled bhatura into the hot oil. Using a slotted spoon or ladle, gently press down on the center of the bhatura. This helps it puff up. As it puffs, flip it over and fry the other side until golden brown and evenly cooked. Each bhatura should take about 30-60 seconds per side.
9. Remove the fried bhatura from the oil and place it on absorbent paper towels to drain excess oil. Repeat with the remaining dough balls.
10. Serve hot Chole with freshly fried Bhature immediately, accompanied by sliced onions, green chilies, and a wedge of lemon.

recipes.title: Classic Rich Chocolate Cake with Chocolate Buttercream
recipes.description: American style Classic Rich Chocolate Cake with Chocolate Buttercream prepared in 75 minutes.

ingredients.name: Hot strong brewed coffee (or boiling water)

instructions: Preheat your oven to 175°C (350°F). Grease and flour two 9-inch round cake pans, or line with parchment paper circles at the bottom.
In a large bowl, sift together the all-purpose flour, granulated sugar, 75g unsweetened cocoa powder, baking soda, baking powder, and 1 tsp salt. Whisk until well combined.
In a separate medium bowl, whisk together the large eggs, buttermilk, vegetable oil, and 2 tsp vanilla extract until smooth.
Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients. Mix on low speed with an electric mixer, or by hand, until just combined. Do not overmix.
Carefully add the hot strong brewed coffee (or boiling water) to the batter. The batter will be thin, this is normal. Stir until fully incorporated.
Divide the batter evenly between the prepared cake pans. Bake for 30-35 minutes, or until a wooden skewer inserted into the center comes out clean. Be careful not to overbake.
Let the cakes cool in the pans for 10-15 minutes before inverting them onto a wire rack to cool completely. Ensure cakes are completely cool before frosting.
While the cakes cool, prepare the chocolate buttercream: In a large bowl, using an electric mixer, beat the softened unsalted butter on medium speed until creamy (about 2-3 minutes).
Gradually add the powdered sugar, about a cup at a time, beating until smooth after each addition. The mixture will be thick.

Add 50g unsweetened cocoa powder, 60ml whole milk (or heavy cream), 1 tsp vanilla extract, and the pinch of salt. Beat on low speed until combined, then increase to medium-high and beat until light, fluffy, and smooth (about 3-5 minutes). If frosting is too thick, add more milk, 1 tablespoon at a time.
Once the cakes are completely cool, place one layer on a serving plate or cake stand. Spread about one-third of the buttercream evenly over the top.
Carefully place the second cake layer on top. Use the remaining buttercream to frost the top and sides of the entire cake, smoothing it with an offset spatula or knife.
Optionally, decorate with chocolate shavings or sprinkles. Slice and serve at room temperature.
